pageTitle: Global AI Governance & Inclusive Education Ecosystem

nav.wordmarkAria: GIEE — Global AI Governance & Inclusive Education Ecosystem, home

footer.tagline: The Global Inclusive Education Ecosystem — designing the global benchmarks, policies, and guardrails that keep AI an instrument for collective empowerment and educational trust.

hero.title:
Global AI Governance &
Inclusive Education Ecosystem
hero.subtitle: The rapid ascension of generative artificial intelligence demands a proactive, cross-sector approach to technology governance—one that prioritizes human capability, institutional accountability, and equity.

overview.p1: The Global Inclusive Education Ecosystem (GIEE) serves as a foundational bridge linking emerging technology with robust human-centered frameworks. In direct collaboration with the Global Online Education Regulator (GOER) and the United Nations-Geneva Forum, we are moving beyond a posture of merely reacting to digital shifts.
overview.p2: Together, we are designing the global benchmarks, policies, and guardrails that ensure AI serves as an instrument for collective empowerment, ethical growth, and educational trust.

pillars.eyebrow: Our Core Pillars of Systemic Governance
pillars.intro: When we discuss “human-centered design” in artificial intelligence architecture, it cannot simply be a rhetorical catchphrase. True technological equity requires an intentional, evidence-based framework. Our ecosystem is anchored in three structural truths:

items.name: Technology Must Amplify Human Capability
items.description: Systems must serve the individual, not the inverse. We advocate for and audit algorithmic models to ensure they are engineered to elevate human curiosity, critical thinking, and baseline capability—never to diminish, gatekeep, or displace the human architecture of learning.

items.name: Equity Demands Intentional Design
items.description: Societies do not stumble into ethical AI platforms. Responsible deployment requires a deliberate vetting infrastructure that forces technology architects, policymakers, and institutions to audit how a digital tool impacts learner privacy, access, and psychological safety before it scales globally.

items.name: Trust is our Ultimate Institutional Currency
items.description: If educators, students, and community stakeholders do not trust the operational systems they rely on, the innovation inherently fails. By anchoring emerging technology in transparent, rigorous governance models, we effectively turn institutional skepticism into collaborative progress.

items.name: The GOER AI Integrity Badge
items.description: Administered by the GOER Board, the AI Integrity Badge represents a critical evolution in educational quality assurance. Far more than a technical validation process, this badge establishes the global baseline for ethical, transparent, and accountable AI integration. By auditing and recognizing technical innovators who meet these stringent criteria, we provide educational systems with verified, safe, and equitable digital tools.

items.name: The UN-Geneva Forum Alliance
items.description: Through our continuous collaboration with the Geneva Forum, GIEE scales localized benchmarks into global policy frameworks. This alliance bridges the gap between grassroots educational technology and high-level international diplomacy, ensuring that global frameworks for digital sovereignty are rooted in data-driven, real-world impact.

cta.heading: Shape the Future of Trust with Us
cta.body: The intersection of AI governance and inclusive education is the defining policy challenge of our era. Whether you are a scholar, practitioner, tech architect, or public servant, your insights are vital to constructing these global guardrails.

partner.heading: Join the Sandbox
partner.intro: GIEE partners with forward-thinking institutions, community advocates, and industry leaders to build bankable, human-centered public assets. Please fill out the form to connect with our Executive Leadership Team.

sandbox.description: We bridge high-level international policy with real-world, grassroots execution—anchored in the GLQF and GOER principles.

collaboration.description: Whether you arrive with a case study, a new proposal, or a research question, our team will route your inquiry to the right Case Study Lead.

portfolio.description: See our active case studies and ecosystem partners on the <portfolio>Research Portfolio</portfolio>.

research.heading: Explore Our Research Portfolio
research.intro: Welcome to the Global Inclusive Education Ecosystem (GIEE) Research Portfolio. GIEE functions as a quality-assured regulatory sandbox, bridging high-level international policy with real-world, grassroots execution. By centering our initiatives around the GLQF and GOER principles, we actively shift global education from standard content delivery to outcome-based competency and human-AI synergy.

initiatives.name: Women in AI Framework
initiatives.description: This initiative explores the structural inclusion of women's perspectives across the entire AI development lifecycle.
partners: Mount Saint Mary's University (Los Angeles, CA)
University of Barcelona — Social Work Department

initiatives.name: Insight Recognition
initiatives.description: An AI-driven assessment and personalized learning environment that maps knowledge through individualized, bottom-up concept mapping.

initiatives.name: Scaling & Expanding Youth-Led Critical AI Education
initiatives.description: A critical science and technology program addressing professional development for K-12 educators regarding sociotechnical harms.
partners: Race, Abolition, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI) Program

initiatives.name: murmur: AI Design in Mental Health
initiatives.description: A structured clinical instrument built on Schema Therapy models to provide clinician-supervised reflective support for learners.

initiatives.name: Street Racket: Schools and Hybrid Learning Environments
initiatives.description: Investigating how movement-based learning can be enhanced through AI-supported reflection and goal-setting.

initiatives.name: CheckIT Learning: AI Supporting SEN Students
initiatives.description: Exploring how neuroscience-informed digital pathways enhance engagement for students with Special Educational Needs (SEN).

communityBand.title: Connect with the Ecosystem Architecture
communityBand.body: The heartbeat of GIEE relies on our active, decentralized peer-to-peer network. Don’t wait for our next formal presentation to step into the ecosystem. Join our official working group right now to interact with our Case Study Leads and participate in public evidence reviews.
communityBand.cta: Join the GIEE LinkedIn Community Group
